title: Skill Discovery, Trigger Descriptions, and Boundaries · Trigger Forensics
summary: Make explicit and implicit invocation reliable without false positives. Learners isolate plausible failures, compare competing hypotheses, and record cause-level repair evidence. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in a trigger evaluation dataset and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Front-load use cases and trigger terms in a concise skill description.
Write diverse prompts that should activate the skill.
Write adjacent prompts that must not activate the skill.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:

stimulus.title: Description Triggers case record
stimulus.caption: Three excerpts from a learner evidence packet; only one connects a claim to a reproducible check.

lines.label: assumption
lines.text: Use a changed debug case for agent ext triggers that tests this competency: Front-load use cases and trigger terms in a concise skill description.. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result.

lines.label: risk
lines.text: The skill name alone controls implicit selection.

lines.label: evidence
lines.text: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Front-load use cases and trigger terms in a concise skill description. Artifact under review: a trigger evaluation dataset.

options.text: The Description Triggers record says a trigger evaluation dataset looks right but supplies no governing condition, observation, changed case, or counterexample. This does not resolve inspect the failure record.

For inspect the failure record, apply Description Triggers without its stated preconditions, omit the a trigger evaluation dataset trace and counterexample, and treat one plausible output as complete proof. This does not satisfy Description Triggers for a trigger evaluation dataset.
The record names the assumption, applies Description Triggers, and reports a changed-case check tied to the stakeholder decision.

steps.title: Implement the cause-level repair
steps.instruction: Append a agent-ext-trigger-evals-d6qug3: manifest entry with concrete verify plus failure, owner, or repair evidence.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Trigger Evals into observable performance evidence.

content.code:
agent-ext-trigger-evals-d6qug3:
  verify: [replace with a deterministic check]
  failure: [replace with the rejected condition]
  repair: [replace with a local reproduction and correction path]
content.caption: Shape of the required increment; replace generic language with the current stakeholder case.
